beats.narration_text: Vanakkam. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of gene drive: one edit floods a million mosquitoes. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of gene drive: one edit floods a million mosquitoes
output: ✓ Gene Drive: One Edit Floods a Million Mosquitoes

beats.narration_text: Gene Drive: One Edit Floods a Million Mosquitoes is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. the core behaviour of gene drive: one edit floods a million mosquitoes. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Gene Drive: One Edit Floods a Million Mosquitoes

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Gene Drive: One Edit Floods a Million Mosquitoes

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Gene Drive: One Edit Floods a Million Mosquitoes. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Drive inheritance rate (d), Starting frequency (p₀). Verify: the key behaviour of gene drive: one edit floods a million mosquitoes. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Gene Drive: One Edit Floods a Million Mosquitoes. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Drive inheritance rate

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Gene Drive: One Edit Floods a Million Mosquitoes — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from gene-drive-spread.html

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Show the core physics loop from gene-drive-spread.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: gene drive: one edit floods a million mosquitoes. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag Drive inheritance rate (d) from 0.5 to 1.0. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of Drive inheritance rate (d) from 0.5 to 1.0

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of Drive inheritance rate (d) from 0.5 to 1.0

beats.narration_text: the prediction holds — gene drive: one edit floods a million mosquitoes behaves as the equations say. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of gene drive: one edit floods a million mosquitoes. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Gene Drive: One Edit Floods a Million Mosquitoes simulation and add a second slider that controls Drive inheritance rate (d). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
